where: backend/main.py (poll-loop wiring, 13-line diff in 0dc5a7e); backend/services/ingestion_service.py (reclaim query honoring cleanup_retry_at)

section: P0.6 Corpus Deletion And Orphan Cleanup
verbatim: Heartbeat/extend the lease while a large chunk or Neo4j purge is active. (live 2026-07-13)
action: Accept receipt commit 0dc5a7e — verified 2026-07-13 (commit message: long purges heartbeat owner-guarded cleanup_lease_until every lease/3; heartbeat-shape test among the 3 new tests in test_storage_lifecycle.py). Re-verify via the heartbeat test in that suite.
who: backend service (ingestion_service purge tasks)
where: backend/services/ingestion_service.py (heartbeat during chunk/Neo4j purge); backend/tests/test_storage_lifecycle.py (heartbeat shape test)
why: Without heartbeat, a purge longer than the lease window gets double-owned by a reclaimer, risking concurrent partial deletes of the same corpus.
feasibility_note: Directly attested by commit 0dc5a7e message and its test additions; the 1.7M-row authentic_library purge executed successfully the same day under this code, which is the strongest practical receipt. Caveat: the purge-duration-beyond-lease SCENARIO test is still missing (see P0L:L493) — the heartbeat unit exists, the adversarial end-to-end case does not.
required_receipt: pytest backend/tests/test_storage_lifecycle.py -k heartbeat → green; plus the L493 purge-beyond-lease scenario test once added.

section: P0.7 Documentation And Claim Integrity
verbatim: Require completion reports to distinguish deployed code, migrated legacy data, and future-only behavior (standing convention recorded in the acknowledgement; the Implementation Log entries follow it, including explicit deploy-pending status).
action: Re-verify by sampling: pick the 3 most recent Implementation Log entries in RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md and confirm each explicitly labels deployed vs migrated vs future-only (S14's final report must also follow this convention per PLAN_CRITIQUE S14 row).
who: junior-agent (spot audit each ledger edit); owner-adjacent convention, enforced by every report author
why: The deployed/migrated/future-only distinction is what prevents 'code exists' from masquerading as 'retrieval improved' — the ledger header's core contract.
feasibility_note: The convention is recorded and the plan critique's C3/C4 findings show it has teeth (6 IN-CODE boxes were correctly NOT marked [x] because undeployed — the convention working as designed). As a standing convention it can never be 'finished', only continuously honored; the S0 deploy-train rule (PLAN_CRITIQUE:75, 'do not hold merged code dark across steps') is its operational enforcement.
required_receipt: Each new Implementation Log entry carries explicit deployed/migrated/future-only labels; S14 final report structured by the same three categories (spot-check, no automated gate exists or is required).

section: P0.7 Documentation And Claim Integrity
verbatim: Require health/readiness claims to identify whether they test process liveness, model load, or real inference (convention recorded in the acknowledgement; the endpoint-level separation itself is tracked as P1.8).
action: Accept the convention as recorded; the enforceable half (endpoint-level liveness/load/inference split on the MLX sidecars) is P1.8, which is IN CODE on wave1/warm and merges at S0 — verify at S0 that the embedder /health warmup status and reranker startup-inference readiness expose the three-way distinction.
who: backend sidecars (embedder_mlx :8082, reranker_mlx :8081) for the P1.8 half; report authors for the convention half
where: docs/RAPTOR_CRITIQUE_ACKNOWLEDGEMENT_2026-07-12.md (convention); P1.8 code on wave1/warm branch (embedder warmup / status split / readiness diag)
why: A 'healthy' sidecar that never loaded its model produces silent vector=0 retrieval — the exact green-health failure mode this convention names (and which project memory has hit twice: dropped override compose, Ghost B wedge).
feasibility_note: Convention recorded; correctly scoped — the box claims only the CONVENTION, explicitly deferring endpoint work to P1.8. Plan-critique receipt: P1.8 shows '3 [x], 3 IN CODE (wave1/warm, pending merge)' and S0 is 'land the dark code'. TERMINOLOGY per registry_readiness: 'readiness' unqualified is banned — qualify as corpus_readiness / inference_ready / retrieval_readiness etc.; this convention is the readiness-family disambiguation rule applied to health claims.
required_receipt: Every future health/readiness claim names its level (liveness | model load | real inference); P1.8's S0 merge verified by probing the sidecar endpoints for the three-way status split.

section: P0.7 Documentation And Claim Integrity
verbatim: Keep durable baseline counts and probe timestamps attached to claims that may become stale (`docs/baselines/` census + latency artifacts, referenced from Implementation Log entries).
action: Re-verify the artifact family exists and is dated: ls docs/baselines/ → BASELINE_2026-07-13.json (census), LATENCY_2026-07-13.json (latency), EVAL_2026-07-13_{qdrant_only,qdrant_mongo,qdrant_mongo_graph}.json (quality), ORPHAN_MANIFEST_2026-07-13.json (done today, all present). Fix the one violation found: the ORPHAN_MANIFEST was overwritten in place by the post-purge re-run — re-commit the pre-purge version under a distinct dated/suffixed filename so both states stay addressable.
who: script (baseline writers: capture_raptor_baseline.py, probe_tier_latency.py, run_heldout_eval.py); junior-agent for the manifest-filename fix
why: Timestamped durable artifacts are what let any later agent re-check a number instead of trusting session memory — the anti-stale-claim mechanism itself.
feasibility_note: All named artifact species verified present today. TERMINOLOGY (registry_eval 'baseline' row, FAILURE MODE a): 'baseline' is three artifact species — census-baseline (BASELINE_*.json), latency-baseline (LATENCY_*.json), eval-baseline (EVAL_*.json); claims must name the species or readers compare counts against recall rates. VIOLATION FOUND: ORPHAN_MANIFEST_2026-07-13.json holds post-purge zeros at HEAD while the ledger cites its pre-purge counts — same filename, opposite content across git history (see L498/L501); one artifact = one state is the fix.
required_receipt: docs/baselines/ listing with all five 2026-07-13 artifact species present + pre-purge manifest re-committed under a _prepurge suffix; future Implementation Log entries reference species-qualified baseline names.

section: P0.7 Documentation And Claim Integrity
verbatim: No repair is called complete while its legacy data migration remains open (Implementation Log discipline; P0.1 boxes stay open until every corpus verifies).
action: Audit the discipline against current evidence: PLAN_CRITIQUE §2 verified P0.1's verify now PASSES all 4 corpora (exit 0) while two P0.1 boxes still carry stale IN PROGRESS tags — the discipline held (boxes stayed open pending verification) but the tags now lag the green verify. Re-run backend/scripts/p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify and restamp the stale tags per standing rule 3.
who: junior-agent (ledger restamp after verify); every report author (ongoing discipline)
why: Prevents the C1-class failure where 'repair complete' claims hide unmigrated legacy rows that silently degrade retrieval.
feasibility_note: The invariant itself has held (no repair was prematurely closed; P0.1 boxes stayed open until corpora verified). The residual issue runs the OTHER direction: tags too conservative, not too eager — PLAN_CRITIQUE C4 and §2 both flag P0.1's two IN PROGRESS tags as stale against today's green verify (V receipt). Closing them is a ledger edit + verify re-run, folded into standing rule 3 compliance, not new work.
required_receipt: p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify → PASS all 4 corpora, exit 0 (already V today per plan critique), with the two stale P0.1 IN PROGRESS tags restamped in the same ledger edit.

section: P0.7 Documentation And Claim Integrity
verbatim: Runtime claims can be reproduced by a credential-free command (`capture_raptor_baseline.py`, `probe_tier_latency.py`, `p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify` — secrets stay in `.env`/container).
action: Re-verify: all three scripts confirmed present today under backend/scripts/ (ls probe); p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify independently re-run today per PLAN_CRITIQUE §2 (PASS, exit 0, all 4 corpora — V receipt). Periodically re-run each and confirm no credential appears in argv or output, secrets loaded from .env/container only.
who: script (the three reproduction commands); junior-agent for periodic re-runs
why: Credential-free reproducibility is what makes every numeric claim in the ledger independently auditable by any agent, which this whole critique workflow depends on.
feasibility_note: All three scripts verified on disk today; the eval-baseline runner (run_heldout_eval.py) follows the same pattern per registry_eval. Consistent with the non-negotiable invariant 'API keys never printed/committed'. This acceptance is inherently continuous — each new runtime claim must ship with its credential-free reproduction command, per the registry_eval note that 'baseline' claims must name their species and writer script.
required_receipt: ls of the three scripts (done 2026-07-13) + p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify exit 0 (V today); every future runtime claim in the Implementation Log cites one of the credential-free writers.
